subject: Ecology
title: Hummingbirds
description: A quick, practical guide to Hummingbirds.

hook.text: Hummingbirds hover in front of flowers, beating wings 80 times per second, drinking nectar.

buildup.text: They pollinate tubular red flowers that are odorless—birds see color but have weak noses.

discovery.text: Their long bills and tongues reach nectar that insects can't, making them exclusive pollinators.

twist.text: Hummingbirds need to eat every 10-15 minutes to fuel their extreme metabolism.

climax.text: Many tropical plants depend entirely on hummingbirds—lose the bird and the plant can't reproduce.

punchline.text: The smallest bird powers the biggest flowers.

quiz.question: Why are hummingbird-pollinated flowers typically red?
options: Birds see color well but have a weak sense of smell
